    Alerts & notifications
    Critical — act now
    DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
    Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
    Open the DATP reading room →
    BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
    Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Open petition letter →
    Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
    Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Open veterans' petition →
    LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
    Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
    Open LDC comment →
    Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
    Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Read background brief →
    HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
    24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
    Open the accessible HTML form preview → Open the HOME-ARP bundle README →
    Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
    Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
    Open the search portal →
    ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
    HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
    Open district audit dashboard →
    249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
    EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
    See district audit →
    WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
    Platform audit · April 2026
    Draft compliance letter →
    High priority
    All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
    ADA document audit · April 2026
    View ADA analysis →
    0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
    Governance gap · §32-1-202
    See audit dashboard →
    Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
    Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
    See audit dashboard →
    Informational
    2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
    CORA tracker · April 2026
    View tracker →
    Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
    Compliance analysis · April 2026
    Read analysis →
    Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
    New · April 2026
    Find my jurisdiction →
    CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
    New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open blotter dashboard →
    Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
    New · Special Districts · April 2026
    Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
    CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
    New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open CBI form rebuild →
    LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
    New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open ADAM dashboard →

    StorySeed Studios · The Dais · Live Browser Audit · April 2026

    CDOLA Open Records Request
    Accessibility Audit & Redesign

    cdola.colorado.gov/open-records-request · Live DOM audit via Chrome extension · WCAG 2.1 AA · Colorado HB 21-1110

    Mostly Compliant — 5 Confirmed Failures · All Fixable in Under 2 Hours
    Audit summary

    What the live tests found

    2

    Level A
    Failures

    3

    Serious /
    Moderate

    12

    Items
    Passing

    2h

    Est. Total
    Fix Time

    Documented failures

    5 confirmed failures — all from live DOM

    Critical
    Accordion buttons missing aria-expanded at page load
    WCAG 4.1.2 · Level A · Drupal accordion.js initializes state lazily

    Live DOM confirms: all 14 accordion buttons render at page load without aria-expanded. The attribute appears after first user interaction via Drupal's collapse JavaScript. A screen reader user tabbing through 13 FAQ buttons hears "What is The Colorado Open Records Act? button" — no state, no indication the button controls collapsible content.

    Live structure confirmed: h2.card-header > div.panel-title > button.accordion-button with aria-controls present but no aria-expanded.

    ❌ Current (page load state)
    <h2 class="card-header panel-heading" id="heading-accordion-44996-1">
  <div class="panel-title">
    <button class="accordion-button"
            href="#collapse-accordion-44996-1"
            aria-controls="collapse-accordion-44996-1"
            >
      What is The Colorado Open Records Act?
    </button>
  </div>
</h2>
    ✓ Required (initial state)
    <button class="accordion-button"
        aria-controls="collapse-accordion-44996-1"
        aria-expanded="false">
  What is The Colorado Open Records Act?
</button>

    See Drupal fix in the code delivery section below — one Twig template edit sets the initial attribute, and a 12-line JS patch handles toggle.

    ⏱ Fix estimate: 30 minutes · Twig template + JS patch
    Critical
    Focus outline invisible on accordion buttons
    WCAG 2.4.7 · Level AA · Computed: outline-width 0px, outline-style none

    Live computed style confirmed: outline: rgb(255,255,255) none 0px on accordion buttons at focus. The global focus ring is white (5px) — appropriate for the dark nav bar but invisible on the white content area where accordion buttons sit.

    Keyboard users tabbing through 13 FAQ items have zero visual focus indication. This is the most impactful keyboard-navigation failure on the page.

    ❌ Current CSS (partial, simplified)
    /* Global focus — white, works on dark nav only */
:focus {
  outline: white 5px auto;
}

/* Accordion buttons sit on white background — white outline disappears */
.accordion-button {
  /* No override — inherits white outline → invisible */
}
    ✓ CSS Fix
    /* Scoped fix: navy outline on white background — 10.6:1 PASS */
.accordion-button:focus,
.accordion-button:focus-visible {
  outline: 3px solid #1a2857;
  outline-offset: 2px;
  box-shadow: none; /* override any inherited ring */
}

/* Also fix skip link target for complete keyboard support */
#main-content {
  outline: none; /* suppress outline on programmatic focus */
}
#main-content:focus-visible {
  outline: none; /* skip link target shouldn't show ring */
}
    ⏱ Fix estimate: 15 minutes · 4 lines of CSS
    Serious
    Skip link target <div> missing tabindex="-1"
    WCAG 2.4.1 · Level A · Skip link may not move focus in Firefox + screen readers

    Live DOM confirms: <div id="main-content"> with no tabindex. Non-native elements do not receive programmatic focus reliably across all browser/AT combinations without tabindex="-1". In Firefox with NVDA, the skip link may scroll visually but not move focus, forcing the user to still tab through the entire navigation.

    ✓ One-attribute fix
    <!-- In page.html.twig or main layout template -->
<div id="main-content" tabindex="-1">
  {{ page.content }}
</div>
    ⏱ Fix estimate: 5 minutes · One attribute in page.html.twig
    Serious
    Radio fieldset missing <legend> in feedback form
    WCAG 1.3.1 · Level A · Fieldset confirmed present, legend absent

    Live DOM confirms: the "Was this content helpful?" feedback form uses <fieldset> (good) but no <legend>. The question text appears as an adjacent generic element, not as a programmatic group label. Screen readers may announce "Yes, radio button, 1 of 2" without the question context.

    ✓ Drupal webform fix
    <!-- webform--content-rating.html.twig or similar -->
<fieldset>
  <legend>Was this content helpful?</legend>
  {{ element.was_this_content_helpful }}
</fieldset>
    ⏱ Fix estimate: 15 minutes · Drupal webform template
    Moderate
    7 footer links open new tabs without announcement
    WCAG 2.4.4 · Level A · Inconsistent — CORA form links handled correctly

    The CORA form links correctly use <span class="visually-hidden">(opens in new window)</span>. However, 7 footer division links (Division of Housing, Division of Local Government, Property Taxation, Board of Assessment Appeals, State Demography Office, Transparency Online, General Notices) open in target="_blank" with no announcement. This is an inconsistency in the same template.

    ✓ Footer link fix pattern (matches existing CORA approach)
    <a href="https://doh.colorado.gov/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
  The Division of Housing
  <span class="visually-hidden">(opens in new window)</span>
</a>
    ⏱ Fix estimate: 20 minutes · 7 footer links in block--footer.html.twig
    What's working

    12 items confirmed passing — from live DOM

    • Skip link present and pointing to valid #main-content target
    • <html lang="en"> — language declared correctly
    • Page title "Open Records Request | Colorado Department of Local Affairs" — descriptive and unique
    • Zero images missing alt attributes — all 4 images correctly labeled
    • Zero unlabeled form inputs — all inputs have associated labels
    • Zero buttons without accessible names
    • Zero links without accessible text
    • Accordion regions have role="region" and aria-labelledby pointing to correct heading IDs
    • Sidebar navigation has aria-current="page" on the current page link
    • CORA form links announce "(opens in new window)" via sr-only text correctly
    • Social media icon links have descriptive aria-label attributes
    • Color contrast passes on H1, H2, CTA button, body text, and nav links — all verified by computed style
    Accessible redesign

    Redesigned page — fixed accordion + open records education

    All 5 failures corrected. Accordion buttons initialize with aria-expanded="false". Focus visible on all interactive elements. New jurisdiction guide helps people submit to the right office — which is the single most common reason CORA requests fail.

    Open Records Request

    DOLA supports governmental transparency and makes every effort to fulfill records requests within three working days. DOLA is not a central repository — requests must go directly to the office that holds the records.

    Where to send your request

    Sending to the wrong office is the most common reason requests are delayed or closed. Use this guide before submitting.

    City / Municipality
    City Records

    If the record involves city police, city utilities, city permits, city council minutes, or any city-run program — submit to the city clerk's office of the relevant city. Colorado Springs: coloradosprings.gov/open-records (opens in new window)

    County
    County Records

    If the record involves county assessor data, county sheriff, county planning, county roads, or the Board of Commissioners — submit to El Paso County or the relevant county directly. El Paso County: elpasoco.com/open-records (opens in new window)

    State — Submit Here
    DOLA & State Agencies

    Submit to DOLA only for records DOLA creates or maintains — Local Government grants, housing programs, property tax data, or State Demography Office records. For other state agencies, submit directly to that agency.

    DOLA Open Record Request Form (opens in new window) →

    Frequently asked questions

    • The Colorado Open Records Act (C.R.S. § 24-72-201 to 206), commonly called CORA, empowers people to understand how Colorado's government functions through the review and inspection of public records. It requires governmental organizations to provide timely access to public records.

      City County State

      CORA applies at all levels of Colorado government — city, county, and state. Each entity receives and responds to its own requests. There is no central repository. If you need city police records, file with the city. If you need county assessor records, file with the county. If you need DOLA records, file here.

    • A "public record" includes records the agency creates or keeps that are relevant to the work it is required or allowed to do by law or official rules. CORA mandates disclosure but does not obligate agencies to generate new records in response to a request.

      City vs. County vs. State: Each government entity only holds its own records. A city holds its own permits, ordinances, and contracts. A county holds its own planning documents, property records, and commissioner minutes. DOLA holds its own grant awards, housing program data, and department correspondence. Requesting records from the wrong entity will result in a "not in our possession" response.

    • Some information is confidential or excluded under CORA. Examples of records that may be withheld:

      • Personal identifying information (names, contact info, SSNs, public assistance status)
      • Work product for elected officials subject to non-disclosure per § 24-72-202(6)(a)(II)
      • Privileged information (e.g. attorney-client privilege)
      • Investigatory records during an active investigation
      • Personnel files (available to the "person in interest" with proof of identity)
      • Trade secrets and confidential commercial data

      City / County note: Exclusions apply equally at all levels. City police investigatory records, county personnel files, and state agency attorney-client communications are all protected under the same CORA provisions.

    • No fee unless the request involves more than two hours of staff time or more than 25 hard-copy pages. Fees per § 24-72-205(5) and (6) C.R.S.:

      • $30 per hour after the first two hours of research (rounded to nearest 0.10 hour)
      • $0.25 per page after the first 25 hard-copy pages
      • DOLA provides a cost estimate before beginning work; a 50% deposit is required
      • If actual cost exceeds estimate, remainder is due before records are released
      • If actual cost is less, DOLA refunds any overpayment

      City vs. County vs. State fees: Fee structures vary by entity. City of Colorado Springs, El Paso County, and state agencies each set their own rates within CORA's limits. Always confirm the specific entity's fee schedule before submitting. Some municipalities charge more or less than DOLA's rates.

    • Use the CORA Online Request Form (opens in new window). Verbal requests are not accepted. Include: your email, a detailed description of the documents sought, date ranges, and your format preference (electronic or hard copy).

      You may also submit by mail to: Colorado Department of Local Affairs, 1313 Sherman St, Room 320, Denver CO 80203, Attn: CORA Administrator.

      Wrong agency? If DOLA doesn't have the record, they'll tell you and attempt to direct you to the right agency. But it's faster to identify the right office first — use the jurisdiction guide at the top of this page.

    • DOLA strives to respond within 3 working days as required by § 24-72-203(3)(b) C.R.S. The clock starts the first working day after a written request is received. Requests received at or after 2 PM are counted as received the next working day.

      • DOLA may contact you within 3 days to clarify a vague or overly broad request — this pauses the clock
      • If extenuating circumstances exist, DOLA may extend up to 7 additional working days
      • If you do not respond to clarification or fee estimates within 5 business days, the request is closed as abandoned

      City vs. County vs. State timelines: The 3-day response requirement applies to all Colorado government entities under CORA. However, cities and counties may have their own internal procedures and extension policies. Check the specific entity's CORA policy page.

    • Broad requests are costlier and slower. Be specific:

      • Narrow by date range (e.g. January 1 – June 30, 2024)
      • Identify specific email addresses or individuals if possible
      • Name the specific document or record type (e.g. "grant award letter for X program")
      • Do not ask DOLA to suggest search terms — that is not permitted
      • DOLA does not create new records or conduct analysis in response to requests

      If you are unsure what records exist, start with a narrow request and follow up. Multiple narrow requests are often faster and cheaper than one broad request.

    Drupal code delivery

    Template fixes — copy-paste ready

    The site runs Drupal with jQuery 3.7.1. Accordion: h2.card-header > div.panel-title > button.accordion-button. Region: div[role=region][aria-labelledby]. Three files cover all 5 failures. 

    {# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  accordion.html.twig  (or field--bootstrap-accordion.html.twig)
  
  KEY FIX: Add aria-expanded="{{ item.active ? 'true' : 'false' }}"
  to each button at server-render time so screen readers know
  the initial collapsed state without waiting for JS.
  
  Location: themes/custom/YOUR_THEME/templates/accordion/
  Or: modules/contrib/bootstrap_accordion/templates/
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #}

{% for key, item in items %}
  <h2 class="card-header panel-heading color__ink"
       id="heading-{{ accordion_id }}-{{ key }}">

    <div class="panel-title">
      <button
        class="accordion-button button{% if not item.active %} collapsed{% endif %}"
        type="button"
        href="#collapse-{{ accordion_id }}-{{ key }}"
        aria-controls="collapse-{{ accordion_id }}-{{ key }}"
        aria-expanded="{{ item.active ? 'true' : 'false' }}"
        id="btn-{{ accordion_id }}-{{ key }}"
      >
        {{ item.title }}
      </button>
    </div>

  </h2>

  <div
    id="collapse-{{ accordion_id }}-{{ key }}"
    role="region"
    aria-labelledby="heading-{{ accordion_id }}-{{ key }}"
    class="panel-collapse collapse{% if item.active %} show{% endif %}"
  >
    <div class="panel-body card-body">
      {{ item.body }}
    </div>
  </div>

{% endfor %}
    /**
 * accordion-a11y.js
 *
 * Drupal behavior that patches the accordion toggle to keep
 * aria-expanded in sync with open/closed state.
 *
 * Location: themes/custom/YOUR_THEME/js/accordion-a11y.js
 * Register in YOUR_THEME.libraries.yml under your base library.
 *
 * This replaces or supplements the existing collapse handler.
 */

(function ($, Drupal) {
  'use strict';

  Drupal.behaviors.accordionA11y = {
    attach: function (context, settings) {

      // Select all accordion buttons in this context
      var $buttons = $(context).find('.accordion-button[aria-controls]');

      // Ensure aria-expanded is set if Twig hasn't done it
      $buttons.each(function () {
        if (!this.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) {
          var targetId = this.getAttribute('aria-controls');
          var isOpen = $('#' + targetId).hasClass('show');
          this.setAttribute('aria-expanded', isOpen ? 'true' : 'false');
        }
      });

      // Toggle aria-expanded on click
      $buttons.once('accordion-a11y').on('click', function () {
        var $btn = $(this);
        var expanded = $btn.attr('aria-expanded') === 'true';
        $btn.attr('aria-expanded', expanded ? 'false' : 'true');
      });

    }
  };

})(jQuery, Drupal);


/**
 * YOUR_THEME.libraries.yml — register the script:
 *
 * base:
 *   version: VERSION
 *   css:
 *     theme:
 *       css/style.css: {}
 *       css/a11y-fixes.css: {}     # add this
 *   js:
 *     js/accordion-a11y.js: {}     # add this
 *   dependencies:
 *     - core/drupal
 *     - core/jquery
 */
    /**
 * a11y-fixes.css
 * Addresses all 5 confirmed failures from the live browser audit.
 * Location: themes/custom/YOUR_THEME/css/a11y-fixes.css
 *
 * FAILURE 1: Accordion aria-expanded — handled in Twig + JS (see other tabs)
 * FAILURE 2: Accordion button focus outline invisible on white bg
 * FAILURE 3: Skip link target missing tabindex (handled in page.html.twig)
 * FAILURE 4: Fieldset legend (handled in webform template)
 * FAILURE 5: Footer new-tab links (handled in block--footer.html.twig)
 */


/* ─── FAILURE 2: Accordion button focus ───────────────────────────── */
/* Current global :focus sets white outline — invisible on white bg.
   Scope a navy override specifically to accordion buttons.
   Navy #1a2857 on white: 10.6:1 — exceeds AA (4.5:1) ✓           */

.accordion-button:focus,
.accordion-button:focus-visible {
  outline: 3px solid #1a2857;
  outline-offset: 2px;
  box-shadow: none;
}

/* Collapsed button state: slightly muted to communicate state */
.accordion-button.collapsed {
  color: #2e3a4a; /* ink-mid — 9.1:1 on white ✓ */
}

/* ─── FAILURE 2 cont: All interactive elements in content area ────── */
/* Catch any other interactive elements on the white content bg        */
#main-content a:focus,
#main-content a:focus-visible,
#main-content button:not(.accordion-button):focus,
#main-content button:not(.accordion-button):focus-visible,
#main-content input:focus,
#main-content input:focus-visible,
#main-content select:focus,
#main-content select:focus-visible,
#main-content textarea:focus,
#main-content textarea:focus-visible {
  outline: 3px solid #1d4ed8; /* focus blue — 6.7:1 on white ✓ */
  outline-offset: 2px;
}

/* ─── FAILURE 3: Skip link target focus suppression ──────────────── */
/* tabindex="-1" must be added in page.html.twig (see Twig tab).
   CSS ensures the main container doesn't show a focus ring itself.   */
#main-content:focus {
  outline: none;
}

/* ─── Skip link — ensure visible on focus ────────────────────────── */
.visually-hidden-focusable:focus,
.skip-link:focus {
  position: static;
  clip: auto;
  width: auto;
  height: auto;
  overflow: visible;
  outline: 3px solid #1d4ed8;
  outline-offset: 2px;
  background: #1a2857;
  color: #ffffff;
  padding: 8px 16px;
  z-index: 9999;
}

/* ─── FAILURE 5: Visually-hidden utility (for new-window notices) ── */
/* Already exists as .visually-hidden on the site. Verify it uses
   this pattern — not display:none or visibility:hidden (those
   also hide from AT). Correct pattern:                               */
.visually-hidden {
  position: absolute;
  width: 1px;
  height: 1px;
  padding: 0;
  margin: -1px;
  overflow: hidden;
  clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);
  white-space: nowrap;
  border: 0;
}

/* ─── Feedback form: fieldset/legend styling ─────────────────────── */
/* Once the legend is added in the webform template, style it         */
.webform-submission-content-rating-form fieldset {
  border: none;
  padding: 0;
  margin: 0;
}
.webform-submission-content-rating-form legend {
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 500;
  color: #1a1a2e;
  padding: 0;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

/* ─── page.html.twig patch (copy into template, not CSS) ─────────── */
/*
  In page.html.twig, change:
    <div id="main-content">
  To:
    <div id="main-content" tabindex="-1">

  This makes the skip link actually move focus in Firefox + NVDA.
*/

    Prepared by StorySeed Studios · The Dais · El Paso County, Colorado · April 2026
    Live browser audit via Chrome extension · jQuery 3.7.1 confirmed on page · Drupal CMS · WCAG 2.1 AA
    This document is for informational purposes. Not legal advice.

    Get email updates

    We'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents whenever this office posts new public-record material.

    By subscribing you agree to receive emails about this topic. We'll send a confirmation link first. Unsubscribe any time. (consent v2026-04-30-v1)

    © 2026 CheetoChopsticks & StorySeed Studios↗ (opens in a new window)  ·  The Dais↗ (opens in a new window)  ·  El Paso County, Colorado  ·  For informational purposes only.  ·  Open source.  ·  Manage your subscriptions
